g.text: Memory Overhead: Flat vs Multi-Level Page Tables

div.p: The Compression Principle Multi-level tables allocate second-level structures on demand. If PDE[5].present == false, the entire 4 MB region (1024 pages) is unmapped — zero bytes allocated for that region's page table. Real processes are sparse: code + heap + stack + a few libraries typically touch <1% of their virtual address space.
All 1024 page regions accessed (Full 4 GB virtual address space mapped)
Dense space: Flat is slightly better (4 MB vs 4.1 MB — 2.5% overhead for indirection)
Only 10 page regions accessed (Code, heap, stack, a few libraries)
Sparse space: Multi-level wins dramatically (44 KB vs 4 MB — 90× reduction!)
